obligations.statement: Rewrite the critical-point singularity definition as exact uniform inequalities and separate assumptions from examples and asserted consequences.
obligations.disposition: The exact rewrite is in the proof map. A globally bounded fixed-centre factor Phi implies localization and weak-L(3/2); bare weak-L(3/2) controls neither separated cores nor anisotropic diameter. The proof-minimal repair states uniform high-level localization directly.

obligations.statement: Define vorticity direction on the zero-vorticity set and preserve the required weighted-BMO bound.
obligations.disposition: The theorem must assume one measurable global unit extension satisfying omega-vector=|omega| xi and the uniform weighted-BMO bound. Smooth divergence-free vorticity does not imply such a modulus, and a zero-set choice made after the estimates is not automatic.

obligations.statement: Verify uniform-in-time profile, Lorentz, and weighted-BMO constants on the exact terminal interval.
obligations.disposition: The repaired hypotheses provide fixed profile, Lorentz, localization, and direction constants on I=(T-star-epsilon,T-star). This is assumed conditional uniformity, not a PDE consequence. The source's later phrase 'entire interval' must be read as I; the downstream threshold check remains O2607-10.

obligations.statement: Check the full tensor Biot-Savart formula and exact unidirectional cancellation for the stretching eigenvalue.
obligations.disposition: The indexed principal-value derivation verifies the componentwise commutator and the zero Rayleigh contraction e^T T(|omega|e)e. The source overstates this at line 218: the full unidirectional strain tensor need not vanish.

obligations.statement: Verify the scale-local Jones extension with global BMO seminorm proportional to phi(R).
obligations.disposition: For the cube seminorm on B_(2R), the global weighted-ball hypothesis gives C M_xi phi(2R). Jones extension on the rescaled unit ball has an R-independent constant. Subtracting and restoring the ball mean tracks additive constants, and equality on both the source support and B_R proves the near-field identity componentwise.

obligations.statement: Verify vector-valued CRW boundedness on weak Lorentz L(3/2,infinity) by a valid interpolation argument.
obligations.disposition: The source's reflexivity wording is false for L(3/2,infinity), but the same scalar CRW commutator is strong on L(4/3) and L2. Real interpolation at theta=1/3 gives the required weak-L(3/2) bound with one BMO factor; the fixed 27-term tensor sum costs only a dimensional constant.

obligations.statement: Reproduce the near, middle dyadic, and far tail estimates with exact kernel and norm powers.
obligations.disposition: The estimate survives for R at most 1/64. The exact kernel rearrangement is (rho^3+s/|B1|)^(-1), the final middle annulus must be truncated, the N+1 mean term retained, and the endpoint weight bounded by 3 phi(R) rather than the source's factor 2. Restriction to B_R cancels the R^(-2) pointwise scale.

obligations.statement: Justify truncated testing of the scalar vorticity-magnitude inequality under the stated regularity and at vorticity zeros.
obligations.disposition: The regularisation (|omega-vector|^2+delta^2)^(1/2) gives the distributional Kato inequality with alpha times |omega| set to zero on the zero set. Positive-level truncation, spatial cutoffs, and temporal regularisation make the whole-space test legal on compact pre-terminal intervals.

obligations.statement: Verify Lorentz real interpolation, Sobolev embedding, Young exponents, and coercive damping.
obligations.disposition: The identity (L(3/2,infinity),L(6,2))_(2/3,1)=L(3,1), Sobolev-Lorentz embedding, Young pair (3/2,3), and the support-sensitive weak-L(3/2) estimate give the stated damped ODE with exact powers.

obligations.statement: Verify a single truncation threshold and all constants remain uniform on the actual pre-singular interval.
obligations.disposition: One threshold can simultaneously enforce profile localization, R at most 1/64, logarithmic comparability, nonlinear absorption, and lambda at least ||omega(t0)||_infinity. All estimates hold uniformly on I=(T-star-epsilon,T-star); the source's claim at line 405 over all of (0,T-star) is replaced by I.

obligations.statement: Invert the logarithmically corrected distribution inequality with explicit cutoffs and one-sided constants.
obligations.disposition: Explicit trial levels and volumes prove both inversions with uniform small-volume and high-level cutoffs. The vorticity exponents (3/2,3/2) become rearrangement exponents (2/3,1), and velocity rearrangement exponents (1/3,1) become distribution exponents (3,3), without using asymptotic equality.

obligations.statement: Verify the exact O'Neil convolution bound, whole-space tail, and transfer of the logarithmic exponent from vorticity to velocity.
obligations.disposition: O'Neil's maximal-rearrangement inequality, the exact |x|^(-2) rearrangement, and the global weak-L(3/2) tail preserve one logarithm for the Biot-Savart representative. For the source's absolute L-infinity velocity, one must additionally fix zero harmonic background or carry a uniformly bounded spatial constant into the high-level cutoff.

obligations.statement: Prove quantitative 3D-to-1D sparseness at a usable scale for every spatial center.
obligations.disposition: At a high peak-relative level, choose r from the uniform velocity-volume upper bound so that delta times |B_r| equals that bound. This gives 3D 3/4-sparseness around every centre at one common radius. Signed polar coordinates and the centred rearrangement of the |tau|^2 weight then give a direction with 1D density at most (3/4)^(1/3) at the same radius.

obligations.statement: Synchronize escape time, evaluation time below T-star, local lifespan, and analytic radius without circular extension.
obligations.disposition: The source's undefined maximal analyticity time is replaced by half the guaranteed local lifespan tau_t=nu/(c1(M)||u(t)||_infinity^2). A first-singular-time lifespan argument forces ||u(t)||_infinity to diverge, last minima supply escape times arbitrarily near T-star, and s=t+tau_t/2 remains below T-star with analytic radius at least c nu/||u(s)||_infinity.

obligations.statement: Verify slit-domain harmonic measure, boundary hypotheses, complexified velocity bounds, and phase handling.
obligations.disposition: For each centre, the correct domain is the connected slit disk D_r minus the compact good real set K. Projecting the holomorphic velocity onto its real centre direction makes the real part harmonic, positive at the centre, bounded by half the escape norm on K, and bounded by M times that norm in the disk. Solynin plus the two-constants principle yields the required pointwise bound.

obligations.statement: Repair the stray source character and restate the origin-in-void branch as a pointwise bound feeding the final supremum.
obligations.disposition: Delete the stray T at source L658. The origin-in-void branch bounds one arbitrary centre and does not alone contradict escape. After both branches give |u(x0,s)| at most ||u(t)||_infinity for every x0, taking the supremum contradicts the strict escape inequality.
